items.title: Fed Has ‘No Tolerance’ for Elevated Inflation, Warsh Says

items.title: Treasuries Rally as Cool CPI Data Cuts July Fed Hike Bets to 20%
items.description: US Treasuries surged as traders pulled back from bets on Federal Reserve interest-rate hikes after consumer prices data came in lower than forecast.

items.title: US CPI Falls for the First Time Since 2020, Core Gauge Flat
items.description: US consumer prices declined in June for the first time in six years and a key gauge of underlying inflation was little changed, taking some pressure off the Federal Reserve to raise interest rates.

items.title: Warsh Says Fed Has ‘No Tolerance’ for Elevated Inflation
items.description: Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh said policymakers at the central bank have no tolerance for high inflation, reiterating a vow to tame price growth that has been elevated for five years.

items.title: Dangote Ends Naira Pricing for Refined Oil Products in Nigeria

items.title: Goldman Breaks Own Stock-Trading Revenue Record Again

items.title: Markets Brace for CPI, Warsh Hearing & Bank Earnings | Bloomberg Brief 07/14/2026

items.title: US Stock Futures Rise as Traders Parse CPI Data, Warsh Comments
items.description: US stock futures rose before the bell on Tuesday as investors parsed latest inflation data and Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh’s remarks.

items.title: US Premarket Movers for July 14, 2026
items.description: S&P 500 Index futures are little changed as of 7:50 a.m. in New York as traders refrained from taking on risk on a busy day that kicked off with Wall Street earnings.

items.title: Persian Gulf’s Oil Tanker Lifeline Takes a Hit
items.description: Renewed missile attacks on ships crossing the Strait of Hormuz threaten to bolster crude’s rally.

items.title: Emerging Bond Returns Beating Treasuries as Carry Trades Boom
items.description: Emerging-market bonds’ outperformance over Treasuries is attracting growing numbers of investors looking to capitalize on the compelling carry-trade returns in the sector.

items.title: JPMorgan Sees Record Profit as Stock-Trading Climbs 86%
items.description: JPMorgan Chase reported its highest quarterly profit ever as the second-quarter equities haul climbed 86% from a year earlier to $6.03 billion. Gerard Cassidy, head of US bank strategy and large-cap b

items.title: Fed Is Behind The Curve as Tech Economy Booms, TS Lombard Says
items.description: The Federal Reserve should start tightening policy to constrain the artificial intelligence boom gripping the US, TS Lombard Chief Economist Freya Beamish said.

items.title: Hormuz Tensions Put Commodity Supplies at Risk Far Beyond Oil
items.description: The latest flare-up of tensions over the Strait of Hormuz is threatening fresh global supply disruptions way beyond oil and gas.

items.title: Thailand Scraps Plan to End Visa-Free Entry for Indian Tourists

items.description: China called for restoring normal passage through the Strait of Hormuz as soon as possible after President Donald Trump reinstated the US blockade of Iranian ships transiting through the vital waterwa

items.title: Rate Hike Chances Rising as Warsh Takes Center Stage
items.description: Following a spike in oil prices and hawkish commentary from Fed officials, traders see a nearly 50% chance of a July rate hike.

items.title: Emerging Assets Steady as Middle East Tensions Spur Caution
